channel.title: Frontion Geopolitical News
channel.description: Geopolitical news curated from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 Israel Issues New Displacement Orders for Southern Lebanon Towns
item.description: Israeli military ordered evacuations for over 10 villages in southern Lebanon, including areas north of the Litani River beyond its current zone of occupation, despite a US-brokered ceasefire.
item.author: Al Jazeera

item.title: 🇮🇱✈️ Israel Approves Purchase of F-35 and F-15IA Fighter Jet Squadrons
item.description: Israel gave final approval to purchase two new combat squadrons of F-35 and F-15IA fighter aircraft from Lockheed Martin and Boeing, part of a $119 billion military buildup plan for the coming decade.
item.author: Straits Times

item.title: 🛢️🚨 Oil Tanker Hijacked Off Yemen Coast Near Somalia
item.description: An oil tanker was hijacked off the coast of Yemen and taken towards Somalia, marking the second such incident in the past 10 days amid ongoing regional instability.
item.author: BBC

item.title: 🇨🇺🇺🇸 Cuba Condemns New US Sanctions as Illegal and Abusive
item.description: Cuba denounced new US sanctions as illegal and abusive, adding to existing US oil blockade measures that have caused widespread blackouts and fuel shortages across the island nation.
item.author: BBC

item.title: 🇺🇸🇩🇪 NATO seeks answers from US on Germany troop cuts
item.description: NATO says it is working with the US to understand details of planned troop reductions in Germany, after Trump announced deeper cuts beyond the initial 5,000 withdrawal.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine strikes Russian Baltic port with drone swarm
item.description: Ukrainian drones hit Primorsk oil terminal near St Petersburg, marking a major escalation as Ukraine extends its reach deep into Russian territory. The port handles significant oil exports.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany Mittelstand shows resilience amid industrial decline
item.description: Small and midsize enterprises (SMEs) remain the backbone of Germanys economy, with hidden champions like Zeiss and Herrenknecht maintaining global leadership in specialized sectors despite broader industrial struggles.
item.author: Deutsche Welle

item.title: 🇲🇲 Myanmar military gains ground but civil war far from over
item.description: The junta has retaken territory and opened political channels after rigged elections installed Min Aung Hlaing as president, but resistance groups still control significant areas and armed opposition persists.
item.author: Philippine Daily Inquirer

item.title: 🇹🇭 Bangkok street food culture under threat from vendor crackdown
item.description: Thailands capital is relocating street vendors from busy districts, raising concerns about livelihoods and the iconic food culture that defines Bangkok.
item.author: SCMP

item.title: 🇳🇿🇸🇬 New Zealand PM Luxon visits Singapore for annual leaders meeting
item.description: Christopher Luxons three-day visit focuses on strengthening the New Zealand-Singapore Comprehensive Strategic Partnership, with agreements on trade in essential supplies.
item.author: Channel News Asia

item.title: 🛢️ Americas energy dominance strategy faces long-term risks
item.description: A Straits Times analysis argues that Trumps approach to energy geopolitics - from sanctions to military action in Iran - may undermine US oil industry competitiveness as other nations accelerate clean energy adoption.
item.author: Straits Times

item.title: 🇨🇳🇮🇷 China issues injunction blocking US sanctions on five refineries
item.description: Chinas Commerce Ministry moved to block US sanctions on five Chinese refiners accused of buying Iranian oil, issuing an injunction through state news agency Xinhua as Beijing pushes back against Washington pressure campaign.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 Senior Republicans break with Trump over Germany troop withdrawal
item.description: Two top Republican lawmakers expressed concern about the Pentagon decision to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany, highlighting bipartisan unease over the timing amid Iran war tensions and tariff disputes straining transatlantic relations.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🇰🇵🇷🇺 North Korea mobilizes youth for war effort alongside Russia
item.description: Kim Jong Un addressed the ruling party youth congress in Pyongyang, emphasizing young people central to domestic mobilization and North Korea military role in Russia war against Ukraine, as the two countries discuss long-term military cooperation through 2031.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Russia and Ukraine give conflicting accounts of Sumy village capture
item.description: Russia Defense Ministry claimed troops seized Myropillia village in Ukraines Sumy region, but Ukrainian military denied the village changed hands, as fighting continues along multiple fronts in the east.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli Military Orders Evacuation of 11 Southern Lebanon Towns
item.description: The Israeli military issued an urgent evacuation order for residents of 11 towns and villages in southern Lebanon, telling them to move at least 1,000 meters away from their homes amid ongoing operations against Hezbollah.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸🇵🇭🇨🇳 US-Philippines Deploy Anti-Ship Missile System Near Taiwan in War Games
item.description: Philippine and US forces showcased the NMESIS anti-ship missile system in Batanes province near Taiwan during annual military exercises, as regional tensions over the self-governed island continue to escalate.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🛢️💰 OPEC+ Agrees Symbolic Oil Output Hike Despite Hormuz Closure
item.description: OPEC+ agreed in principle to a small oil output quota hike, though the increase will remain largely symbolic until shipping through the Strait of Hormuz can resume amid the ongoing Iran conflict.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🇮🇷💸 Millions of Jobs Lost as Iran Battles "Operation Economic Fury" Fallout
item.description: A fragile ceasefire may have paused the US-Israeli war on Iran, but the economic cost is devastating Tehran, with millions of jobs lost and crippling effects on daily life from the sustained military and economic pressure campaign.
item.author: Al Jazeera

item.title: 🇮🇳🇦🇪 Modi's UAE visit signals new era for India's Middle East policy
item.description: Prime Minister Modi's upcoming UAE stopover represents a strategic opportunity for India to establish a key pivot in the Persian Gulf, similar to Singapore's role in Southeast Asia. The visit underscores the Emirates importance in India's regional calculations.
item.author: The Week

item.title: 🇺🇸🇩🇪 US Troop Drawdown from Germany Signals Shift in European Defense Responsibilities
item.description: Germany's defense minister says the US withdrawal of 5,000 troops should spur Europe to take greater defense responsibility, as Trump-Pentagon tensions with Europe mount over Iran war and tariffs. Trump signaled cuts could go "a lot further."
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🇮🇷⚔️ Iran Standoff Could Leave Trump Worse Off Than Before War Began
item.description: Reuters analysis finds the US-Iran conflict has failed to deliver a decisive military or diplomatic victory, with Tehran's blockade strategy and Trump's stalled negotiations leaving both sides in an uneasy stalemate.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: ⛪🇮🇷 Jailed Iranian Nobel Laureate Narges Mohammadi in Hospital After Cardiac Crisis
item.description: Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi's health has "deteriorated seriously" in Iranian custody, with the Norwegian Nobel Committee calling for her release to receive proper medical care.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🇹🇲🌍 Reclusive Turkmenistan Shows Signs of Cautiously Opening to the World
item.description: The isolated Central Asian nation is taking tentative steps toward economic modernization and international engagement, with new tech startups and easing restrictions signaling a shift from decades of isolation.
item.author: Reuters

item.title: 🇸🇦🇹🇷🇦🇪 Saudi Arabia, Turkey and UAE propose new trade corridors bypassing Hormuz
item.description: Middle Eastern governments are reviving decades-old pipeline proposals and drawing up new rail-sea transport corridors as a structural shift away from dependence on vulnerable Persian Gulf shipping routes. The plan would route cargo from UAE and Oman ports through Saudi Arabia to Jordan, then to Egypt's Suez or Syrian ports.
item.author: SCMP
